heading: Pay for Sunday and holiday work
label: 5546 Pay for Sunday and holiday work
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1966ActDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 922, .June 30, 1945, ch. 212, § 30259 Stat. 298, .May 24, 1946, ch. 270, § 1160 Stat. 218, .Sept. 1, 1954, ch. 1208, § 20768 Stat. 1110, , .July 18, 1958Pub. L. 85–533, § 172 Stat. 377In subsections (a) and (b), the word “officer” is omitted as included in “employee”.In subsections (b) and (c), the word “designated” is substituted for “such a” and “such” in former section 922(b) and (c) to identify the holiday as one designated by statute, Executive order, or the Board of Commissioners of the District of Columbia.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

1967ActSection of title 5Source (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)5546(a)5 App.: 921a., , (c), .July 18, 1966Pub. L. 89–504, § 405(b)(1)80 Stat. 297In subsection (a), the words “An employee who performs work . . . is entitled to pay . . . at the rate of his basic pay” are coextensive with and substituted for “Any . . . service . . . performed . . . shall be compensated . . . at the rate of basic compensation of the officer or employee performing such work.” The words “” are substituted for “section 201 of this Act” to reflect the codification of that section in title 5, United States Code. The words “between midnight Saturday and midnight Sunday” are coextensive with and substituted for “within the period commencing at midnight Saturday and ending at midnight Sunday”.section 5542(a) of this title

Amendments1998—Subsec. (a). inserted at end “For employees serving outside the United States in areas where Sunday is a routine workday and another day of the week is officially recognized as the day of rest and worship, the Secretary of State may designate the officially recognized day of rest and worship as the day with respect to which the preceding sentence shall apply instead of Sunday.”Pub. L. 105–2771968—Subsec. (b). , substituted “District of Columbia Council” for “Board of Commissioners of the District of Columbia”.Pub. L. 90–623, § 1(13)(A)Subsec. (d). , substituted “5542(a)” for “5442(a)”.Pub. L. 90–623, § 1(13)(B)

L. 105–61, title VI, § 636111 Stat. 1316Oct. 10, 1997

children.label: a
children.text: An employee who performs work during a regularly scheduled 8-hour period of service which is not overtime work as defined by a part of which is performed on Sunday is entitled to pay for the entire period of service at the rate of his basic pay, plus premium pay at a rate equal to 25 percent of his rate of basic pay. For employees serving outside the United States in areas where Sunday is a routine workday and another day of the week is officially recognized as the day of rest and worship, the Secretary of State may designate the officially recognized day of rest and worship as the day with respect to which the preceding sentence shall apply instead of Sunday.section 5542(a) of this title
overtime work as defined by .section 5542(a) of this title

children.label: c
children.text: An employee who is required to perform any work on a designated holiday is entitled to pay for at least 2 hours of holiday work.

children.label: d
children.text: An employee who performs overtime work as defined by on a Sunday or a designated holiday is entitled to pay for that overtime work in accordance with .section 5542(a) of this titlesection 5542(a) of this title

children.label: e
children.text: Premium pay under this section is in addition to premium pay which may be due for the same work under section 5545(a) and (b) of this title, providing premium pay for nightwork.
